data.body:
PASS 1 review (T3 — Integration feasibility, overlay): NOT-APPLICABLE → PASS, no findings.

T3 applies only when a plan integrates an external API/CLI/service/library or asserts a previously-unused capability. Cluster 1 is a pure internal DRY consolidation onto an existing internal seam (`src/rebar/_store/canonical.py`) using only stdlib `json`/`hashlib`. There is no external endpoint/subcommand to verify and no auth/HTTPS surface. Verified against the codebase:

- The target seam `src/rebar/_store/canonical.py` exists and today exposes the positional `canonical_str(event)`/`canonical_bytes(event)` the plan describes (defaults `ensure_ascii=False, sort_keys=True, separators=(",",":")`), so the "additive keyword-only params" extension is achievable as described.
- Import-path feasibility for the two reconciler migration targets is confirmed: sibling modules `inbound_translate.py` and `batch_dispatch.py` (same `rebar_reconciler` package as `conflict_resolver.py`/`mutation.py`) already do `from rebar._store.canonical import canonical_str` — the seam is reachable from those modules.
- All four migration sites match the plan's descriptions: `signing.py::_canonical_payload` (`ensure_ascii=False`), `llm/workflow/schema.py::canonical_json`/`content_hash` (`ensure_ascii=False`), `conflict_resolver.py::_hash_value` (`json.dumps(..., sort_keys=True, default=str, separators=(",",":"))` — no `ensure_ascii`, i.e. stdlib default True, matching the plan's `ascii_only=True, default=str` requirement), and `mutation.py::serialize_manifest` (`ensure_ascii=True`, matching `ascii_only=True`).
- All six "untouched" existing importers named in the plan are real and import positionally.

No capability gap; no SPIKE warranted. Clean for T3.
